title: Periodic Foams and Manifolds
speaker: Frank Lutz
abstract: WORKSHOP ON TOPOLOGY: IDENTIFYING ORDER IN COMPLEX SYSTEMShttp://www.ias.edu/math/files/WorkshopIntro.pdf

proof_stages.label: From periodic foams to TCP triangulations
proof_stages.summary: Natural periodic Voronoi foams with Plateau-type cells motivate the dual Delaunay/TCP triangulations whose allowed dual cells correspond combinatorially to edge valences five and six with a no-adjacent-six condition.

proof_stages.label: Small-valence test problem
proof_stages.summary: Known results giving triangulations of all closed orientable 3-manifolds with bounded edge degree are used to motivate the sharper small-valence question, first tested through the two-dimensional Euler-characteristic warm-up.

proof_stages.label: Enumeration and recognition machinery
proof_stages.summary: Backtracking enumeration under local valence constraints is paired with practical manifold recognition by simulated annealing with bistellar flips, justified abstractly by Pachner’s theorem but used heuristically in computations.

proof_stages.label: Classification at edge valence at most five
proof_stages.summary: The computation yields exactly 4,787 triangulated 3-manifolds with edge valence at most five, all spherical, and this fits an independent curvature theorem and higher-dimensional analogues forcing positive-curvature-like join products.

proof_stages.label: Reverse valence and TCP homology-sphere construction
proof_stages.summary: The reverse question of all edge valences at least five is true for 3-spheres but false for homology spheres, as shown by modifying an icosahedral/dodecahedral triangulation of the Poincaré homology sphere to an 18-vertex TCP triangulation.

proof_stages.label: F-vector constraints and Walkup Gamma
proof_stages.summary: Basic f-vector equations, complete-graph and lower-bound restrictions, and Walkup’s theorem are organized into the renormalized topological invariant Gamma, later interpreted through h- and g-vectors.

proof_stages.label: G2-minimal search bounds
proof_stages.summary: G2-minimal and g2-irreducible triangulations impose strong vertex-link restrictions and finite search regions, enabling computations that determine further Gamma values and show Gamma is at least 21 outside the listed manifolds.

proof_stages.label: Gamma as 3-manifold complexity
proof_stages.summary: The talk closes by comparing Gamma with Matveev complexity, emphasizing finiteness and subadditivity properties and posing the additivity/equality question for connected sums.
